findings.issue: PDF p.25 carries usage stats — 'Buy Plan pipeline: 3,492 shortlisted-to-buy options', 'Trends: 1,598 (46%) | Yousta: 836 (85%) | Azorte: 385 (43%) | Integrated with Cortex + PLM' — that the page does NOT surface anywhere. The §03 'How Many People Use It' chip strip was deleted per critical-changes; the underlying usage facts disappeared with it. Apex reading PDF p.25 will notice the page-omits scale-of-use proof.
findings.fix: Either (a) reinstate a single-line stat strip after §02 with the four chips ('3,492 options in pipeline · Trends 1,598 (46%) · Yousta 836 (85%) · Azorte 385 (43%)') OR (b) document the deletion in the spec with a sentence on why the partial-rollout numbers were judged not-yet-Apex-ready.

findings.issue: Phrase 'the substrate for the L2 → L3 jump on Costing in the autonomy framework' uses 'substrate' (jargon — exec audience won't parse) and references the autonomy framework without the §06 Progress section to anchor the L2→L3 move (that section was deleted). Reads as orphan jargon now.
findings.fix: Replace 'is the substrate for the L2 → L3 jump on Costing in the autonomy framework' with 'is the foundation for higher autonomy on Costing — see /autonomous/ for the framework'. Or drop the autonomy reference entirely now that §06 Progress is gone.

findings.issue: Hero is missing the parenthetical sub-line that intelliverse and pulsepoint carry under H1 (the small italic 'descriptor line' — e.g. '(Procuro · IntelliMake · IntelliQC · IntelliPack)'). Costing Engine has H1 → strap → audience pills with no sub-line. Asymmetric vs siblings.
findings.fix:
Add a sub-line under H1, e.g. '

(Bill of Materials · vendor RFQ · cost-sheet)

' — or accept the asymmetry if intentional. Minor.

findings.issue: Card 1 caption 'Costing review and approval dashboard' maps to PDF p.27. PDF p.27 caption is 'Costing review and approval dashboard with cost breakdowns by style' — page caption appends 'Cost breakdowns by style with brick, season, fashion grade, option, hit, issue date, assigned PM and stage.' — page is more verbose than source. Fine, but slight drift from verbatim convention.
findings.fix: Optional: trim card 1 caption to match PDF more closely, OR keep the current expansion as it adds reader-useful detail. Document the expansion choice if challenged.

strengths: Tight, factual page — no banned vocabulary detected, no exclamation marks, no emojis, no engineering jargon in stat positions. Tone-of-voice check passes cleanly.
Screenshot deduplication is correctly applied — 3 unique cards (027 review-dashboard, 028 BOM-breakdown, 028-b activity-log) with no repeat of the BOM line-items view that PDF p.26/p.28 duplicate.

Caption swap (between 028-v2 and 028-v2-b) verified correct: card 2 'Detailed BOM cost breakdown' = PDF p.28 caption; card 3 'Costing activity log' = PDF p.28 activity-log image. Matches the critical-changes notes.
Section renumbering (§04 What improved → §03; §06 Screenshots → §04) is contiguous and correct after the §03 Buy Plan Pipeline + §04 Updates Shipped + §06 Progress deletions.
Cross-page consistency clean — /impetus/ index card 05 reads 'Costing Engine · Automated material-level costing with vendor pricing & approval workflows' which matches hero strap exactly.
